19 |publisher = 新編岡崎市史編さん委員会 |page = |ref = 新編史料民俗19 }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20 |publisher = 新編岡崎市史編さん委員会 |page = |ref = 新編総集編20 }} == 関連項目 == * [[滝山寺]] * [[日本の祭一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Takisanji Oni Matsuri}} * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 滝山寺鬼まつり｜岡崎市観光協会公式サイト] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|瀧山寺　鬼まつり（チャンネルおかざき　平成28年3月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきさんし おにまつり}} [[Category:岡崎市の祭り]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岡崎市の歴史]] [[Category:鬼]]

{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=滝山寺鬼まつり |英文表記= |種類=[[火祭り]] |画像=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |画像サイズ=250px |画像説明=鏡餅を持つ孫面の冠面者 |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=旧正月7日に近い土曜日
（[[2023年]]（[[令和]]5年）は2月11日） |初回開催=[[鎌倉時代]] |会場=[[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]] |主催=滝山寺鬼まつり保存会[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス=シャトルバス、臨時バスが運行 |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} '''滝山寺鬼まつり'''（たきさんじ おにまつり）は、[[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]]で行われている[[火祭り]]。 == 概要 == [[重要文化財]]である[[滝山寺]]本堂に巨大な松明を30数本持ち込むことから天下の[[奇祭]]とも称される『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 302頁。。愛知県の無形[[民俗文化財]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}愛知県の指定名称は「滝山寺鬼祭」である。。 == 歴史 == 起源は[[鎌倉時代]]、[[源頼朝]]の祈願に始まると伝えられている。室町時代に一時廃絶。祖父[[徳川家康]]に対し畏敬の念が強かった[[徳川家光]]は[[寛永]]18年（[[1641年]]）9月17日、滝山寺に対し[[朱印地・黒印地|朱印地]]412石を与えた{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }}。[[正保]]3年（[[1646年]]）10月18日には家光の命により[[滝山東照宮]]が創建され『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 231-232頁。、さらに正保4年（[[1647年]]）、家光は学頭の青龍院亮盛に、毎年滝山寺において天下泰平の祈願をするよう命じた。以後、鬼まつりは[[徳川幕府]]の行事として盛大に行われるようになった{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }}。 [[明治維新]]後、徳川幕府の庇護を失い、神仏分離が影響して[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）に休止となるも、[[1888年]]（明治21年）、滝村の地元住民らが費用を捻出するなどして復活した森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109)　家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、8面。。火祭りで登場する3つの鬼の面は室町時代前半期の作とされている。 [[1944年]]（[[昭和]]19年）と[[1945年]]（昭和20年）は[[灯火管制]]のため中止された。[[1969年]]（昭和44年）も、滝山寺本堂の屋根替え（[[檜皮葺]]）が同年1月中旬から始まったため中止となった『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止　滝山寺　本堂の屋根替えで」。。 [[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）は2月11日に開催森田真奈子「男衆ら完全燃焼 邪気払う鬼まつり 岡崎」 『中日新聞』2017年2月14日付朝刊、西三河版、25面。。 [[2021年]]（[[令和]]3年）、[[2022年]]（令和4年）は、[[新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)|新型コロナウイルス]]感染対策で一般観覧を中止し、関係者のみで行った{{cite news |author= |url= https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 | title= 炎赤々、邪気退散　岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 | newspaper= 中日新聞 | date= 2021-2-14 | accessdate = 2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書| author=横田沙貴 | url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php | title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる | publisher=東海愛知新聞 | date=2023-2-9 | accessdate=2023-2-10 }}。 [[2023年]]（令和5年）は2月11日に開催。3年ぶりに一般観覧者を受け入れた。 == ギャラリー == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg| 岡崎市滝町の[[滝山寺]] |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg| [[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）頃の滝山寺鬼まつり |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg| 当日15時。滝山寺三門から行列が出立。 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg| 大松明・十二人衆行列 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg| 冠面者（かんめんじゃ）が身につける祖父面、祖母面、孫面 |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg| 祖父面、祖母面、孫面。祖父面をかぶっているのは[[カクキュー]]の第17代の早川久右衞門。岡崎趣味会の関係者らが岡崎公園内で撮影（1922年） }} == 祭りの内容 == 祭り当日の行事は、（1）大松明・十二人衆行列、（2）仏前法要、（3）鬼塚供養、（4）庭祭り、（5）火祭りに大別される。庭祭りは田遊び祭りとも呼ばれ、[[田楽]]の一種である。 もともとは旧正月元旦から始まる修正会（しゅしょうえ）の結願日に当たる旧正月7日の晩に行われてきたが、現在は旧正月7日に近い土曜日に行われている。 現地に一般参詣者用の駐車場はないため、当日は[[真伝町]]の[[岡崎市龍北総合運動場]]に臨時駐車場が設けられる。同運動場から滝山寺まで[[名鉄バス]]の有料シャトルバスが運行。また、[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[東岡崎駅]]から滝山寺までの臨時バスも増発される[http://www.meitetsu-bus.co.jp/info/detail/443 滝山寺（岡崎市）「鬼まつり」開催に伴う臨時運転について<2月11日>｜名鉄バス]。 === 準備 === 鬼まつりの中心となる十二人衆は「庭祭り」用の大松明を作成する。大松明に使われる竹は[[丹坂町]]から調達する。その他の松明は十二人衆が所有する山の竹やぶから調達され、滝山寺鬼まつり保存会の会員が中心となって作成する。松明作りは滝山寺三門近くの広場で行われる。 当日の1週間から10日ほど前に主役の3人の冠面者（かんめんじゃ）の発表がなされる。近年、祖父面と祖母面は地元の成人男性から、孫面は地元の[[岡崎市立常磐小学校|常磐小学校]]の男子児童から選ばれている{{cite news | author =竹内雅紀| url =http://www.fmokazaki.jp/tokai/150211.html | title =冠面者3人決まる　21日・岡崎　天下の奇祭　滝山寺鬼まつり | newspaper = [[東海愛知新聞]] | date= 2015-2-11 | accessdate =2017-1-29}}{{cite news | author =| url =http://www.fmokazaki.jp/tokai/120204_2.html | title =祖父面・鷲見さん　祖母面・宇野さん　孫面・小林君　滝山寺鬼まつり　冠面者決まる　岡崎で11日 | newspaper = 東海愛知新聞 | date= 2012-2-4 | accessdate =2017-1-29}}{{cite news | author =今井亮| url =http://www.fmokazaki.jp/tokai/110203.html | title =冠面者決まる　祖父・浅川さん　祖母・中根さん　孫・野中くん　12日「滝山寺鬼まつり」 | newspaper = 東海愛知新聞 | date= 2011-2-3 | accessdate =2017-1-29}}。冠面者は祭りまでの7日間、斎戒沐浴して別室で起居し、[[四肢動物|四足動物]]の肉を口にしないなどの戒律がある。炊事なども男の手によってなされる[http://takisanji.net/oni.html 瀧山寺（滝山寺）岡崎市・天台宗吉祥陀羅尼山薬樹王院 | 鬼まつり]。 地元の[[岡崎市立常磐中学校|常磐中学校]]の生徒は、鬼の顔をかたどった[[土鈴]]を製作し、滝山寺の節分会と鬼まつりで販売する伝統がある（収益はユニセフに募金）[http://www.oklab.ed.jp/weblog/tokiwa/2015/02/post_95.html 岡崎市立常磐中学校: 土鈴販売と鬼祭り（2015年2月21日）]。 === 当日 === {{DEFAULTSORT:たきさんし おにまつり}} [[Category:岡崎市の祭り]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岡崎市の歴史]] [[Category:鬼]]

revision.comment: 外部リンクの修正 https:// -> web.archive.org (www.chunichi.co.jp) ([[Wikipedia:Bot|Bot]]による編集)
revision.text:
{{イベントインフォメーション |イベント名称=滝山寺鬼まつり |英文表記= |種類=[[火祭り]] |画像=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |画像サイズ=250px |画像説明=鏡餅を持つ孫面の冠面者 |通称= |正式名称= |旧名称= |開催時期=旧正月7日に近い土曜日
（[[2023年]]（[[令和]]5年）は2月11日） |初回開催=[[鎌倉時代]] |会場=[[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]] |主催=滝山寺鬼まつり保存会[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |共催= |後援= |協賛= |企画制作= |協力= |運営= |プロデューサー= |出展数= |来場者数= |会場アクセス名= |最寄駅= |直通バス=シャトルバス、臨時バスが運行 |駐車場= |URL= |特記事項= }} '''滝山寺鬼まつり'''（たきさんじ おにまつり）は、[[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[滝町 (岡崎市)|滝町]]で行われている[[火祭り]]。 == 概要 == [[重要文化財]]である[[滝山寺]]本堂に巨大な松明を30数本持ち込むことから天下の[[奇祭]]とも称される『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 302頁。。愛知県の無形[[民俗文化財]]に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}愛知県の指定名称は「滝山寺鬼祭」である。。 == 歴史 == 起源は[[鎌倉時代]]、[[源頼朝]]の祈願に始まると伝えられている。室町時代に一時廃絶。祖父[[徳川家康]]に対し畏敬の念が強かった[[徳川家光]]は[[寛永]]18年（[[1641年]]）9月17日、滝山寺に対し[[朱印地・黒印地|朱印地]]412石を与えた{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }}。[[正保]]3年（[[1646年]]）10月18日には家光の命により[[滝山東照宮]]が創建され『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 231-232頁。、さらに正保4年（[[1647年]]）、家光は学頭の青龍院亮盛に、毎年滝山寺において天下泰平の祈願をするよう命じた。以後、鬼まつりは[[徳川幕府]]の行事として盛大に行われるようになった{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }}。 [[明治維新]]後、徳川幕府の庇護を失い、神仏分離が影響して[[1873年]]（[[明治]]6年）に休止となるも、[[1888年]]（明治21年）、滝村の地元住民らが費用を捻出するなどして復活した森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109)　家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、8面。。火祭りで登場する3つの鬼の面は室町時代前半期の作とされている。 [[1944年]]（[[昭和]]19年）と[[1945年]]（昭和20年）は[[灯火管制]]のため中止された。[[1969年]]（昭和44年）も、滝山寺本堂の屋根替え（[[檜皮葺]]）が同年1月中旬から始まったため中止となった『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止　滝山寺　本堂の屋根替えで」。。 [[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）は2月11日に開催森田真奈子「男衆ら完全燃焼 邪気払う鬼まつり 岡崎」 『中日新聞』2017年2月14日付朝刊、西三河版、25面。。 [[2021年]]（[[令和]]3年）、[[2022年]]（令和4年）は、[[新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)|新型コロナウイルス]]感染対策で一般観覧を中止し、関係者のみで行った{{cite news |author= |url= https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 | title= 炎赤々、邪気退散　岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 | newspaper= 中日新聞 | date= 2021-2-14 | accessdate = 2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書| author=横田沙貴 | url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php | title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる | publisher=東海愛知新聞 | date=2023-2-9 | accessdate=2023-2-10 }}。 [[2023年]]（令和5年）は2月11日に開催。3年ぶりに一般観覧者を受け入れた。 == ギャラリー == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg| 岡崎市滝町の[[滝山寺]] |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg| [[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）頃の滝山寺鬼まつり |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg| 当日15時。滝山寺三門から行列が出立。 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg| 大松明・十二人衆行列 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg| 冠面者（かんめんじゃ）が身につける祖父面、祖母面、孫面 |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg| 祖父面、祖母面、孫面。祖父面をかぶっているのは[[カクキュー]]の第17代の早川久右衞門。岡崎趣味会の関係者らが岡崎公園内で撮影（1922年） }} == 祭りの内容 == 祭り当日の行事は、（1）大松明・十二人衆行列、（2）仏前法要、（3）鬼塚供養、（4）庭祭り、（5）火祭りに大別される。庭祭りは田遊び祭りとも呼ばれ、[[田楽]]の一種である。 もともとは旧正月元旦から始まる修正会（しゅしょうえ）の結願日に当たる旧正月7日の晩に行われてきたが、現在は旧正月7日に近い土曜日に行われている。 現地に一般参詣者用の駐車場はないため、当日は[[真伝町]]の[[岡崎市龍北総合運動場]]に臨時駐車場が設けられる。同運動場から滝山寺まで[[名鉄バス]]の有料シャトルバスが運行。また、[[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[東岡崎駅]]から滝山寺までの臨時バスも増発される[http://www.meitetsu-bus.co.jp/info/detail/443 滝山寺（岡崎市）「鬼まつり」開催に伴う臨時運転について<2月11日>｜名鉄バス]。 === 準備 === 鬼まつりの中心となる十二人衆は「庭祭り」用の大松明を作成する。大松明に使われる竹は[[丹坂町]]から調達する。その他の松明は十二人衆が所有する山の竹やぶから調達され、滝山寺鬼まつり保存会の会員が中心となって作成する。松明作りは滝山寺三門近くの広場で行われる。 当日の1週間から10日ほど前に主役の3人の冠面者（かんめんじゃ）の発表がなされる。近年、祖父面と祖母面は地元の成人男性から、孫面は地元の[[岡崎市立常磐小学校|常磐小学校]]の男子児童から選ばれている{{cite news | author =竹内雅紀| url =http://www.fmokazaki.jp/tokai/150211.html | title =冠面者3人決まる　21日・岡崎　天下の奇祭　滝山寺鬼まつり | newspaper = [[東海愛知新聞]] | date= 2015-2-11 | accessdate =2017-1-29}}{{cite news | author =| url =http://www.fmokazaki.jp/tokai/120204_2.html | title =祖父面・鷲見さん　祖母面・宇野さん　孫面・小林君　滝山寺鬼まつり　冠面者決まる　岡崎で11日 | newspaper = 東海愛知新聞 | date= 2012-2-4 | accessdate =2017-1-29}}{{cite news | author =今井亮| url =http://www.fmokazaki.jp/tokai/110203.html | title =冠面者決まる　祖父・浅川さん　祖母・中根さん　孫・野中くん　12日「滝山寺鬼まつり」 | newspaper = 東海愛知新聞 | date= 2011-2-3 | accessdate =2017-1-29}}。冠面者は祭りまでの7日間、斎戒沐浴して別室で起居し、[[四肢動物|四足動物]]の肉を口にしないなどの戒律がある。炊事なども男の手によってなされる[http://takisanji.net/oni.html 瀧山寺（滝山寺）岡崎市・天台宗吉祥陀羅尼山薬樹王院 | 鬼まつり]。 地元の[[岡崎市立常磐中学校|常磐中学校]]の生徒は、鬼の顔をかたどった[[土鈴]]を製作し、滝山寺の節分会と鬼まつりで販売する伝統がある（収益はユニセフに募金）[http://www.oklab.ed.jp/weblog/tokiwa/2015/02/post_95.html 岡崎市立常磐中学校: 土鈴販売と鬼祭り（2015年2月21日）]。 === 当日 === 15時、「大松明・十二人衆行列」開始。滝山寺三門から本堂に向かい出立。行列には冠面者や十二人衆、住職等が参列する。この時、「滝山寺鬼まつりの唄」が歌われ、町に祭りの始まりを告げる。青木川沿いに行列は進み、唄の合間や道中休憩後の出立時にはほら貝が鳴らされる。行列が本坊へ到着すると、十二人衆は[[精進料理]]の饗応を受ける。現在、この料理は祭りの見学者にも供されている（要予約）。饗応の後、鐘楼の鐘と拍子木の音が十二人衆の本堂への登山を知らせる。 17時、「仏前法要」開始。 18時20分、十二人衆は、滝山東照宮と日吉山王社で長刀（なぎなた）御礼振り等、様々な所作を行う。 18時30分、法要の途中、本堂西側にある鬼塚の前で住職による「鬼塚供養」（豆まき）が行われる。[[2016年]]（平成28年）2月13日の鬼まつりは、この豆まきに[[徳川将軍家|徳川宗家]]当主、[[徳川恒孝]]が参加した[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8WuTs4Vckok 岡崎市広報Youtube/瀧山寺　鬼まつり（チャンネルおかざき「街ぶらり～岡崎発見隊」　平成28年3月1日～10日放送）]。 19時、「庭祭り」開始。十二人衆の中でも上役に位置する東次郎と西次郎が長刀御礼振りで悪魔を払った後、同じくコツボネ、福太郎が鍬を担いで登場する。ここで田遊びの所作が演じられる。内容は田打ち・代かき・苗代作り・種まき・田植えなどの農作業の様子である。太鼓の音に合わせて十二人衆によって、この祭り独自の田植え歌が披露される。 19時45分頃、「火祭り」開始[http://takisanji.net/gyoji.html 瀧山寺（滝山寺）岡崎市・天台宗吉祥陀羅尼山薬樹王院 | 行事案内]。滝山寺の内陣では半鐘、双盤、太鼓が連打され、ほら貝が吹き鳴らされる。この音と共に燃え盛る大松明を持った男達と祖父面・祖母面・孫面を被った3鬼が本堂の外陣と回廊を駆け巡る。孫面の鬼は、初めは右手にまさかり、左手に松明を持って登場するが、途中から豊作を意味する丸餅を持って現れる。大松明を持ち走る人々、鳴り響く音、炎の熱気に包まれ、祭りは最高潮を迎える。大役の拍子木とともに火が消され、一斉の音と光が止む。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書 |author= |title=瀧山寺鬼祭り民俗文化財調査報告書 |publisher=岡崎市教員委員会 |date=2018年3月28日 |isbn= }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1984-9-30 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 史料 民俗 19 |publisher = 新編岡崎市史編さん委員会 |page = |ref = 新編史料民俗19 }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = 新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20 |publisher = 新編岡崎市史編さん委員会 |page = |ref = 新編総集編20 }} == 関連項目 == * [[滝山寺]] * [[日本の祭一覧]] == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Takisanji Oni Matsuri}} * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 滝山寺鬼まつり｜岡崎市観光協会公式サイト] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|瀧山寺　鬼まつり（チャンネルおかざき　平成28年3月1日～10日放送）／岡崎市広報}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たきさんし おにまつり}} [[Category:岡崎市の祭り]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:岡崎市の歴史]] [[Category:鬼]]

revision.text:
{{Event Information |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Demons]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

{{Event Information |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Demons]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:new pages]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

revision.text:
{{Event Information |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Demons]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Demons]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Demons]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

{{Event Information |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Demons]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

revision.text:
{{Event Information |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Demons]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Demons]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Demons]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

{{Event Information |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Demons]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Demons]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

{{Event Information |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Demons]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

{{Event Information |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Demons]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Demons]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]

It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:Festivals in Okazaki]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:History of Okazaki]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:Demons]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:滝山寺鬼まつり]]
