In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]

{{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Takisanji Oni Festival]][[Category:11]] {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takisanji Oni {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takisanji Oni It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |Image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |Image Size=250px |Image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takisanji Oni Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon {{Infobox_holiday |Event Name=Takisanji Oni Matsuri |English Name= |Type={{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} |image=Okazaki-Takisanji-3.jpg |image Size=250px |image Description=Shinto performer with kagami mochi |Common Name= |Formal Name= |Former Name= |Event Period=Saturday closest to the 7th day of the Lunar New Year
([[2023]] was held on February 11) |First Held={{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}} |Venue={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}{{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}} |Organizer=Takisanji Oni Matsuri Preservation Society[http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kensitei/0799.html 滝山寺鬼祭【たきさんじおにまつり】 | 文化財ナビ愛知] |Co-organizer= |Sponsor= |Support= |Planning and Production= |Cooperation= |Management= |Producer= |Exhibitors= |Visitors= |Access= |Nearest Station= |Direct Bus=Shuttle buses and temporary buses available |Parking= |URL= |Notes= }} '''Takisanji Oni Matsuri''' (滝山寺鬼まつり) is a {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} held in {{ill|Taki-cho|ja|滝町}}, {{ill|Okazaki|ja|岡崎市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. == Overview == The festival is renowned as one of the most unusual {{ill|Fire Festival|ja|火祭り}} events in Japan, where over 30 giant torches are carried into the Main Hall of {{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}}, a designated {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}.『[[#新編史料民俗19|新編 史料 民俗 19]]』 p.302. It is designated as an intangible {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財}} of Aichi Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1300/1304/1332/p011771.html | title=岡崎市指定文化財目録 | publisher=岡崎市ホームページ | date=2023-7-12 | accessdate=2024-1-17 }}The official designation by Aichi Prefecture is "Takisanji Oni Matsuri." == History == The festival is said to have originated in the {{ill|Kamakura Period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, initiated as a prayer ceremony by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}. It was briefly discontinued during the {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}. In 1641, {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}} granted the temple 412 koku of red seal land.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/03dai1sho.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第1章 岡崎市の歴史的風致形成の背景 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=52 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The following year, he ordered the construction of {{ill|Takisan Tōshō-gū|ja|滝山東照宮}} and mandated annual prayers for national peace.『[[#新編総集編20|新編 岡崎市史 総集編 20]]』 pp.231-232. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, the festival was suspended due to the loss of Tokugawa patronage and the effects of {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. It was revived in 1888 by local residents.森田真奈子「わたしが編集長 (109) 家康尊崇 維新後も続く」 『[[Chunichi Shimbun|中日新聞]]』2018年4月22日付朝刊、西三河版、p.8. The masks used in the festival date to the early {{ill|Muromachi Period|ja|室町時代}}.{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1184/1169/p020134_d/fil/06dai2sho_takisanji.pdf |title=『岡崎市歴史的風致維持向上計画』第2章 03 滝山寺鬼祭りにみる歴史的風致 |publisher=岡崎市ホームページ |page=170-180 |date=2023-7-14 |accessdate=2024-1-17 }} The festival was canceled in 1944 and 1945 due to wartime blackout restrictions and again in 1969 during roof renovations of the Main Hall.『東海新聞』1968年12月12日、2面、「鬼まつり来年は中止 滝山寺 本堂の屋根替えで」。 In 2021 and 2022, the festival was held privately due to {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行 (2019年-)}} precautions.{{cite news |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210213203125/https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/201940 |title=炎赤々、邪気退散 岡崎で奇祭「鬼まつり」 |newspaper=中日新聞 |date=2021-2-14 |accessdate=2021-2-14 }}{{Cite web|和書|author=横田沙貴 |url=http://fm-egao.jp/tokai/230209.php |title=滝山寺鬼まつり 3年ぶりの一般観覧 冠面者決まる |publisher=東海愛知新聞 |date=2023-2-9 |accessdate=2023-2-10 }} The festival resumed public viewing in 2023. == Festival Events == The festival is divided into five parts: 1. {{ill|Torch Procession|ja|大松明・十二人衆行列}} 2. Buddhist Ritual 3. Demon Mound Memorial Service 4. Garden Festival 5. Fire Festival The Garden Festival, a type of traditional {{ill|Dengaku|ja|田楽}}, reenacts agricultural scenes. == Gallery == {{Gallery |width=200 |height=150 |File:Takisan-ji Temple Hondou, Yamagomori Taki-cho Okazaki 2022.jpg|{{ill|Takisanji|ja|滝山寺}} in Okazaki |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-9.jpg|Takisanji Oni Matsuri circa 1932 |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-4.jpg|Torch procession departing from the Sanmon Gate |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-5.jpg|Giant torches and procession |File:Okazaki-Takisanji-8.jpg|Grandfather, Grandmother, and Grandchild masks used in the festival |File:OKazakishi-no-konjaku-11.jpg|Masks from 1922 featuring cultural leaders }} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [https://okazaki-kanko.jp/event/677 Takisanji Oni Matsuri | Okazaki Tourism Official Website] * {{YouTube|8WuTs4Vckok|Takisanji Oni Matsuri (Channel Okazaki, February 2016)}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takisanji Oni
